surface.name: Redis Cloud quick start
surface.selection_basis: Redis calls Redis Cloud the fastest deployment option, tells new users to start with the Redis Cloud quick start, and says the quick start gets a new user up and running by creating a free database and connecting to it.

alternatives_considered.name: Redis Open Source installation routes
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The general page presents multiple operating-system and container installation routes, while explicitly calling Redis Cloud the fastest deployment option and marking its quick start as the place to start if new.

alternatives_considered.name: Redis Enterprise Software and Kubernetes quick starts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are separate self-managed deployment surfaces; the official general page recommends the Redis Cloud quick start to a new user.

alternatives_considered.name: Use-case quick starts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Data structure, document, vector, agent, and RAG guides begin after the deployment and connection milestone selected by the general documentation.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a free managed Redis database and connect to it using the official new-user path.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected Redis.
The developer does not yet have a Redis Cloud account or database.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A free Redis Cloud database is active and has been opened through one of the quick start's documented connection methods.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: On the first fully specified route for the free Essentials database, selecting Launch Redis Insight web opens Redis Insight in a new browser tab for that database.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The quick start's stated second learning outcome is Connect to your database, and its browser route ends when Redis Insight opens in a new tab.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: It is the last stated learning outcome of the selected quick start. Loading sample data, using Insights, executing use-case tutorials, importing data, and database operations appear as optional or later material.

prerequisites.requirement: An email address, or a Google or GitHub identity, is required to create and activate a Redis Cloud account.
No local software is required for the browser Redis Insight route. The desktop, redis-cli, and client-library branches add their own software prerequisites.
The selected path creates a Free 30 MB Essentials database; the quick start does not document a payment-card requirement for this path.

primary_path.action: Open the Redis getting-started documentation and follow Redis Cloud quick start.
details: Redis Cloud is described as the fastest way to deploy Redis.
The general page marks this quick start with Start here if you're new.
primary_path.success_signal: The page lists Create an account and a free database, then Connect to your database.

primary_path.action: Open the Redis Cloud Sign up page.
primary_path.success_signal: The form and social signup options are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented before form submission.

primary_path.action: Create the account with the form or sign up with Google or GitHub.
details: For the form route, enter the requested information and select Get Started.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The account remains unavailable until the activation email arrives and is used.

primary_path.action: Open the Redis activation email and select Activate account.
primary_path.output: Activated Redis Cloud account and Create your database page
primary_path.success_signal: The Redis Cloud console opens the database-creation page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or delayed email blocks activation; the quick start does not state a delivery time or retry process.

primary_path.action: Keep the Free plan selected on the Create database page.
details: The guide routes existing account holders to an Essentials database guide instead.
Flex, other Essentials, and Pro databases are separate branches.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting another database type changes the documented path.

primary_path.action: Accept the generated database name or change the Database name field.

primary_path.failure_or_wait: The quick start does not prescribe one version.

primary_path.action: Choose a Cloud vendor and Region.
details: The cloud-vendor choices are AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.
The guide links a supported-regions reference.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Available regions depend on the cloud vendor.
Redis documents a brief processing pause before the details page appears.

primary_path.action: Wait for the database status to change from pending to active.
details: A green circle containing a checkmark means active.
primary_path.success_signal: The status indicator becomes a green circle with a checkmark.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide states only that the pause is brief and supplies no retry action.

primary_path.action: In Configuration details, go to Security and reveal the Default user password.
details: Select Default user, then Configure, then the eye icon to view the masked password.
primary_path.success_signal: The default username and password are available for connection.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without the credentials, non-browser connection methods cannot authenticate.

primary_path.action: Select Connect to open the connection wizard.
details: The wizard offers Redis Insight, redis-cli, and Redis client methods.

primary_path.action: Under Redis Insight, select Launch Redis Insight web.
details: The browser route is available only for Essentials databases, which includes the free database created by this path.
The same launch action is also available from the database page under View and manage data with Redis Insight.
primary_path.input: Connection wizard for the free Essentials database
primary_path.success_signal: Redis Insight opens in a new tab for the database.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Other database types require installing Redis Insight on a computer.

branches.condition: The developer uses the form instead of Google or GitHub.
branches.path: Enter the requested information and select Get Started; then continue through the required email activation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds manual form entry but reaches the same database-creation page.

branches.condition: The developer already has an account, already has a database, or wants Flex, paid Essentials, or Pro.
branches.path: Use the linked Essentials, database-management, Flex, or Pro guide instead of the new-account Free plan flow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes provisioning, billing, and possibly browser Redis Insight availability, so it is outside the selected new-user path.

branches.condition: The developer uses Redis Insight desktop.
branches.path: In the connection wizard select Download, install Redis Insight for Windows, macOS, or Linux, select Open in desktop, then approve the browser pop-up by selecting Open Redis Insight. The application connects to the database.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a local download, operating-system installation, link-handler prompt, and desktop application before the same connection milestone.

branches.condition: The developer uses a Redis client library.
branches.path: Choose one of the connection-wizard snippets for .NET with StackExchange.Redis or NRedisStack, Node.js with node-redis, Python with redis-py, Java with Jedis or Lettuce, Go with go-redis, or PHP with Predis. Follow that client's own installation and syntax documentation, then use the generated connection information.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a language and client choice plus client-specific installation and execution. The quick start does not demonstrate one universal terminal output for these clients.

branches.path: Install Redis, build redis-cli from source, or run redis-cli in Docker; use the connection details from Redis Cloud. The CLI documentation shows host and port flags or a redis or rediss URI, recommends REDISCLI_AUTH for passwords, and demonstrates PING returning PONG as a connected command result.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds installation and command-line credential handling before a PONG connection signal.

friction_gates.description: A new account requires form or social signup followed by activation through email.
The developer must choose a database version, cloud vendor, and supported region; no defaults are prescribed by the quick start.
Database creation has a processing pause and pending state before the active checkmark appears.
Database connection credentials are protected behind Default user, Configure, and password-reveal controls.
The connection wizard offers GUI, CLI, and multiple language-client routes with different installation and execution requirements.

sources.title: Open Source: Get started with Redis Open Source
Redis Cloud quick start
evidence_supported: Complete ordered account and free-database path

sources.title: Redis CLI
evidence_supported: Remote host, port, password, URI, and TLS connection options

uncertainties.question: Does Redis intend one connection method as the canonical default?
uncertainties.impact: The browser, desktop, CLI, and client routes have different developer actions and friction.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quick start lists methods and fully specifies the eligible browser route first, but does not label any method as recommended or default.

excluded_after_success: Loading sample data in Redis Insight
Executing data-structure, document, vector, RAG, or AI use-case guides
Managing, securing, backing up, or monitoring the database
